title: Isla and the Lost Bunny
synopsis: When Isla finds a scared little bunny lost in her backyard, she feels worried but uses her brave heart to help find its family, discovering that being kind and patient helps solve problems and makes new friends.

pages.text: Isla was playing in her sunny backyard when she heard a tiny sound. Sniff, sniff, sniff! Behind the big sunflower, she found a small brown bunny. The bunny looked very scared and sad. "Oh no," said Isla softly. "Are you lost, little friend?"

illustration.description: A young Filipino girl with shoulder-length black hair in a bright yellow sundress kneels beside a tall sunflower. A small brown bunny with droopy ears sits nearby, looking worried with big teary eyes. The backyard has green grass, colorful flowers, and a wooden fence. Warm golden sunlight filters through the scene with soft shadows.
illustration.alt: Isla discovering the scared bunny behind a sunflower in her backyard

pages.text: The bunny's whiskers trembled and its little nose twitched. Isla felt worried too, but she wanted to help. "Don't be scared," she whispered gently. "I will help you find your family." The bunny's ears perked up just a little bit when it heard Isla's kind voice.

illustration.description: Close-up view of Isla sitting cross-legged on the grass, extending her hand slowly toward the brown bunny. Isla has a gentle, caring expression with warm brown eyes. The bunny still looks nervous but curious, with one ear slightly raised. Soft green grass surrounds them with dandelions scattered around. The scene has warm, comforting colors.
illustration.alt: Isla reaching out gently to comfort the frightened bunny

pages.text: Isla thought hard about what to do. She remembered her mama always said, "When someone needs help, we use our brave hearts." Even though Isla felt a little scared about talking to a wild bunny, she decided to be brave and help her new friend.

illustration.description: Isla stands up with determination, one hand on her heart and the other pointing forward. She has a brave, confident expression with a small smile. The bunny sits nearby, watching her hopefully. In the background, other backyard animals like a blue jay on the fence and a butterfly near some purple flowers suggest a welcoming neighborhood ecosystem.
illustration.alt: Isla standing bravely, ready to help the bunny find its family

pages.text: Isla looked all around her yard. "Let's check by the berry bushes," she said to the bunny. They walked together slowly. The bunny hopped on wobbly legs behind Isla. Near the blueberry bush, they heard happy chittering sounds. "Listen!" said Isla. "Do you hear that?"

illustration.description: Isla leads the way toward a cluster of blueberry bushes, looking back encouragingly at the small bunny who follows with small, uncertain hops. The yard shows a diverse neighborhood with houses of different colors in the background. A Black child can be seen playing in the neighboring yard. The blueberry bushes are full and green with tiny blue berries.
illustration.alt: Isla and the bunny walking together toward the berry bushes

pages.text: Behind the bushes, they found a mama bunny and two other baby bunnies! They all looked very happy to see the lost little one. The mama bunny twitched her nose as if to say "thank you" to Isla. The baby bunny ran to its family, no longer scared but excited and joyful.

illustration.description: A heartwarming reunion scene with the mama bunny (larger, with nurturing eyes) touching noses with the baby bunny while two sibling bunnies hop excitedly around them. Isla watches with a big, happy smile and claps her hands together. The berry bushes frame the scene with dappled sunlight creating a magical, warm atmosphere with golden and green tones.
illustration.alt: The bunny family reunited while Isla watches happily

pages.text: Isla felt so happy and proud! She had been brave and helped solve a problem. Before hopping away, the little bunny looked back at Isla and wiggled its nose. Isla waved goodbye to her new bunny friends. "Come visit anytime!" she called. Her heart felt warm and full of joy.

illustration.description: Isla waves enthusiastically as the bunny family hops away toward a gap in the wooden fence. The little bunny she helped turns back to look at her one more time. Isla's face glows with happiness and pride. The scene includes her diverse neighborhood with an elderly Latino man watering plants next door and an Asian family's garden visible through another fence gap. Warm afternoon light bathes everything in golden hues.
illustration.alt: Isla waving goodbye to the bunny family as they head home

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Isla feel when she first found the bunny?
questions.explanation: Isla felt worried when she saw the scared bunny, but she wanted to help anyway.

questions.question: What did Isla decide to be when helping the bunny?
questions.explanation: Isla remembered to use her brave heart, just like her mama taught her.

questions.question: Where did they find the bunny's family?
questions.explanation: The bunny family was hiding behind the blueberry bushes in Isla's yard.

questions.question: How did Isla feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Isla felt happy and proud because she helped solve the problem and made new friends.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.text: Isla found a small _____ bunny behind the sunflower. The bunny looked very _____ and sad.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.text: Isla decided to be _____ and help her new friend. They found the bunny family by the _____ bushes.
